reason: Closed-book solvability failure: options E and B rely on the paper-specific definition, threshold, and time-history of the explosion index (XFED>1 for 1211 s pre-runaway vs <1), which is not provided in the stem. Without XFED being defined or any pre-runaway vs post-runaway quantitative hazard metric stated, a test-taker cannot determine E (or refute B) using only the stem plus general engineering reasoning.

MCQ with papers provided. Fails closed-book solvability and is ambiguous: option C (labeled Correct) asserts that different SOC functional forms across two different chemistries/protocols imply different physical mechanisms, which cannot be logically derived from the stem alone (and is not uniquely true; exponential vs linear dependence can arise from the same underlying mechanism under different variable transforms or regimes). It also requires cross-study mechanistic interpretation beyond general reasoning, making the correctness dependent on paper-context assumptions.

MCQ; papers provided. The stem contains all necessary quantitative facts (86°C self-heating onset, >35 h lead time, 31 kJ heating vs 60 kJ stored energy, venting ~180°C SOC-independent in heat ramp) and the correct options (A, D, E) follow from general engineering reasoning plus those stated facts without requiring paper-only definitions. Incorrect options are not defensible under the stem (B asserts an unjustified energy channel; C overgeneralizes protocol-specific venting behavior to warning time; F contradicts the given energy magnitudes). No correct-labeled statement is contradicted by the evidence bundle.

Short-answer; papers provided. The stem contains all necessary quantitative facts (35–75% mass-loss spread at fixed 100% SOC; 35 percentage-point average increase over 25–100% SOC) to identify the reasoning error (unsupported dismissal of systematic trigger-method variation as uncertainty / unjustified assumption that SOC must dominate) and to conclude, via magnitude comparison, that trigger method influences mass loss comparably to SOC. This is non-ambiguous and not contradicted by the evidence (which explicitly supports the key comparison and conclusion).
